feed.title: Google Cloud: Identity-Aware Proxy
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: Identity-Aware Proxy

entry.content:
You can configure custom OAuth clients in Identity-Aware Proxy by using the Google Cloud console; the feature is generally available (GA). You must use custom OAuth clients to do the following:

  • Configure IAP for users who are outside of an organization.
  • Customize the OAuth consent screen with custom branding.
  • Provide default OAuth clients for inherited applications across all IAP-protected resources at the organization or project level.

For more information, see Use custom OAuth clients with IAP.
entry.summary: Identity-Aware Proxy (IAP) now supports custom OAuth clients, allowing for enhanced configuration for external users, custom branding on consent screens, and default client settings at the organization or project level. This feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Custom OAuth clients now available in IAP

entry.content:
The ability to use a path wildcard in the aud (audience) field when using a service account JWT to authenticate with an IAP-secured resource is generally available.

For more information, see Authenticate with a service account JWT.
entry.summary: You can now use a path wildcard in the `aud` field with service account JWTs for IAP-secured resources.
entry.title: Feature: Path wildcard support for service account JWT authentication

entry.content:
The ability to use a path wildcard in the aud (audience) field when using a service account JWT to authenticate with an IAP-secured resource is generally available.

For more information, see Authenticate with a service account JWT
entry.summary: You can now use a path wildcard for the audience field when authenticating with a service account JWT. This feature is generally available and simplifies authentication to IAP-secured resources.
entry.title: Feature: Path wildcard in audience field for service account JWT authentication is now generally available
